message:
fix(safety): replace unsafe byte casts with bytemuck and harden argument parsing

- Replace unsafe `align_to`/`from_raw_parts` in vector_index byte cast
  helpers with safe `bytemuck::try_cast_slice` and `bytemuck::cast_slice`
- Add `bytemuck` dependency with derive feature for Pod/Zeroable traits
- Add tests for byte cast rejection of invalid lengths and misalignment
- Add roundtrip test for f32/f16 byte cast helpers
- Replace panicking `split_once('=').unwrap()` in CLI argument parser
  with defensive `let-else` pattern (br-2iyqp)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1lwxf","title":"Phase 4K: Clipboard integration via OSC52","description":"Leverage ftui-extras/clipboard for OSC52 clipboard integration that works over SSH and in any modern terminal. Add clipboard operations: (1) Copy selected result path+line to clipboard (Ctrl+Y or y), (2) Copy detail pane content to clipboard (Ctrl+C when in detail view without selection), (3) Copy code block from detail view (click or keybinding on specific code block), (4) Copy search query to clipboard, (5) Paste from clipboard into search bar (Ctrl+V -- currently handled by crossterm Paste event, but ftui clipboard provides a unified API). OSC52 is the escape sequence that allows terminal apps to read/write the system clipboard without any external dependency -- it works over SSH sessions which is critical for cass multi-machine search. The clipboard module handles terminal capability detection and graceful fallback.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:21:52.504734512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:33.027618302Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:33.027591773Z","close_reason":"Content preserved in new 2noh9.3 bead: Clipboard OSC52 integration.  {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2hi4u","title":"Phase 4L: HTML/SVG export of TUI view","description":"Leverage ftui-extras/export module to add TUI-to-HTML and TUI-to-SVG export. The export module can render a Buffer snapshot as self-contained HTML or SVG preserving all colors and formatting. This is different from the existing export-html (which exports conversation content as HTML) -- this exports the actual TUI screenshot. Add: (1) Export current TUI view as HTML via command palette (captures exactly what the user sees), (2) Export as SVG for high-quality images (useful for documentation, presentations), (3) Export as plain text (stripped ANSI), (4) Auto-export on asciicast stop (generate an HTML thumbnail of the final frame). This lets users capture beautiful screenshots of their cass TUI for sharing, documentation, or bug reports without needing external screenshot tools.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:22:03.832892321Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:33.337704047Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:33.337678229Z","close_reason":"Content preserved in new 2noh9.4 bead: HTML/SVG TUI export.  {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2hp2","title":"Remove stale #[allow(dead_code)] from MessageGroup types in renderer.rs","description":"The MessageGroup types (MessageGroupType, ToolResult, ToolCallWithResult, MessageGroup) in renderer.rs have #[allow(dead_code)] annotations that were added during initial development before integration. These types are now actively used by group_messages_for_export() and render_message_groups(). Remove the stale annotations so the compiler can properly warn about actually dead code.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T01:09:24.110703932Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T01:13:54.703610399Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T01:13:54.703523858Z","close_reason":"Removed 8 stale #[allow(dead_code)] annotations from MessageGroupType, ToolResult, ToolCallWithResult, and MessageGroup types and their impl blocks. Kept targeted #[allow(dead_code)] on 3 genuinely unused utility functions (render_code_block, render_inline_code, render_links) with clear comments.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2ieo","title":"T4: Additional E2E Test Coverage - Comprehensive Workflows","description":"# Epic: Expand E2E Test Coverage

## Goal
Add comprehensive E2E tests for all major workflows, especially error scenarios and edge cases.

## Current Coverage
- 71 Rust integration test files
- 11 Playwright browser specs
- Performance test suite

## Gaps Identified
1. Error recovery scenarios
2. Mobile device testing
3. Offline mode testing
4. Large dataset handling
5. Concurrent user scenarios
6. Accessibility compliance (partial)

## Approach
- Add dedicated error scenario tests
- Expand mobile device coverage
- Test offline/online transitions
- Add stress tests for large datasets

## Dependencies
- Should align with T3 logging standards","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T04:22:36.738605759Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T05:59:23.928669980Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T05:59:23.928586154Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2ieo","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-30qc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:40Z","created_by":"import"}]}
+{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2iyqp","title":"Fix panicking unwrap on argument parsing in lib.rs","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-14T01:19:03.025323726Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T01:28:40.563196048Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T01:28:40.563157206Z","close_reason":"Fixed: replaced split_once('=').unwrap() with let-else pattern for defensive argument parsing. Also fixed rustfmt issues in vector_index.rs. All gates green.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2jb85","title":"Reliability: fail fast on non-zero SSH script exits in install/index runners","description":"Harden SSH command execution reliability by failing fast on non-zero script exits in install and index runners. Current helper behavior can suppress failing statuses and return stdout, which masks real failures and creates confusing downstream behavior.

User impact:
1. False-success flows that fail later with low-quality diagnostics.
2. Harder incident triage for remote install and indexing problems.
3. Wasted retries and operator time due to hidden root causes.

Scope: src/sources/install.rs and src/sources/index.rs.","design":"1. Refactor SSH helper to return structured execution outcome with exit status, stdout, stderr tail, timeout state, and elapsed duration.
2. Treat non-zero exit status as error by default; allow specific call sites to explicitly handle expected non-zero semantics when needed.
3. Standardize error conversion so user-facing failures include command phase and actionable context.
4. Align logging format with security hardening work so diagnostics are consistent across remote operations.
5. Preserve existing successful-path behavior and output parsing for zero-exit commands.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Non-zero SSH exits in install and index paths fail immediately with typed errors that include phase and exit status.
2. Expected polling behavior is explicitly modeled at call sites and no longer depends on implicit helper suppression.
3. No successful-path regression in existing install/index workflows; no feature loss.
4. Comprehensive unit tests cover zero exit, non-zero exit, timeout, ssh transport failure, and stderr-rich error output.
5. Comprehensive integration or E2E tests validate that failing remote scripts abort flow early with clear diagnostics.
6. E2E logs include host, step, attempt number, timeout budget, exit status, and stderr snippet for quick triage.","notes":"Coordinate with host-key hardening design to avoid duplicate helper abstractions. Run full quality gates plus SSH source integration tests with verbose structured logging enabled.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:25:45.950089454Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T06:33:30.310628988Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T06:33:30.310604653Z","close_reason":"run_ssh_command() in both install.rs and index.rs now fails fast on any non-zero exit code, returning the exit code and stderr in the error. Previously the fallthrough returned stdout even on failures, masking script errors. All 202 sources tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2jb85","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2779d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:53:52.784653357Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2jpl","title":"Add metrics to e2e_cli_flows.rs","description":"## Priority 2: Add Performance Metrics to e2e_cli_flows.rs

### Current State
tests/e2e_cli_flows.rs uses PhaseTracker but doesn't emit performance metrics.

### Required Changes

1. **Add E2ePerformanceMetrics import:**
```rust
use util::e2e_log::{..., E2ePerformanceMetrics};
```

2. **Capture timing for each CLI command:**
```rust
let start = Instant::now();
let output = Command::new(cass_bin())
    .args(&["search", "authentication", "--robot", "--limit", "10"])
    .output()?;
let duration = start.elapsed();

tracker.metrics("cass_search", &E2ePerformanceMetrics {
    duration_ms: duration.as_millis() as u64,
    items_processed: Some(parse_result_count(&output.stdout)),
    memory_bytes: None,
    throughput_per_sec: None,
});
```

### Suggested Metrics
| Command | Metric Name | What to Capture |
|---------|-------------|-----------------|
| cass search | cass_search | duration, result_count |
| cass index | cass_index | duration, sessions_indexed |
| cass view | cass_view | duration |
| cass stats | cass_stats | duration |
| cass health | cass_health | duration |

### Files to Modify
- tests/e2e_cli_flows.rs

### Testing Requirements (CRITICAL)

1. **Verify metrics in JSONL:**
```bash
E2E_LOG=1 cargo test --test e2e_cli_flows -- --nocapture
cat test-results/e2e/*.jsonl | jq 'select(.event == "metrics" and .test.suite == "e2e_cli_flows")'
```

2. **Verify metrics have required fields:**
```bash
cat test-results/e2e/*.jsonl | jq 'select(.event == "metrics") | {name, duration_ms: .metrics.duration_ms, items: .metrics.items_processed}'
```

3. **Verify metrics align with schema:**
```bash
# All metrics must have: name, metrics.duration_ms
cat test-results/e2e/*.jsonl | jq 'select(.event == "metrics" and (.metrics.duration_ms == null or .name == null)) | "INVALID: \(.)"'
# Should return empty
```

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Key CLI commands have timing metrics
- [ ] items_processed captured for search commands
- [ ] Metrics follow E2ePerformanceMetrics schema
- [ ] All metrics appear in JSONL output
- [ ] Metrics values are reasonable (not 0, not negative)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:20:39.226119058Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T19:52:18.375163289Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T19:52:18.375096114Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added E2ePerformanceMetrics to setup_indexed_env (cass_index with throughput), search_basic (cass_search with hit count), view_command (cass_view), health_command (cass_health), stats_command (cass_stats)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
